lede: A look at January 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ali al-Rida and Jonas Armstrong, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2011 Alexandria bombing
events.desc: A bomb explodes as Coptic Christians in Alexandria, Egypt, leave a new year service, killing 23 people.

events.title: Adam Air Flight 574
events.desc: Adam Air Flight 574 breaks apart in mid-air and crashes near the Makassar Strait, Indonesia, killing all 102 people on board.

events.title: World Trade Organization
events.desc: The World Trade Organization comes into being.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches Operation Bodenplatte, a massive, but failed, attempt to knock out Allied air power in northern Europe in a single blow.

events.title: Ellis Island
events.desc: Ellis Island begins processing immigrants into the United States.

events.title: Lachlan Macquarie
events.desc: Major-General Lachlan Macquarie officially becomes Governor of New South Wales.

events.title: The Times
events.desc: The first edition of The Times of London, previously The Daily Universal Register, is published.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Burning of Norfolk – Norfolk, Virginia, is burned to the ground by combined Royal Navy and Continental Army action.

events.title: Bouvet Island
events.desc: Bouvet Island, the world's remotest island, is discovered by French explorer Jean-Baptiste Charles Bouvet de Lozier.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Herr Gott, dich loben wir, BWV 16, his church cantata for New Year's Day to a libretto by Georg Christian Lehms.

births.name: Alassane Ouattara
births.line: Ivorian economist and politician, President of the Ivory Coast

deaths.text: Earth, Wind & Fire, American musician and songwriter
Grace Hopper, American computer scientist and admiral, co-developed COBOL
Joachim du Bellay, French poet and critic
AD 138, adopted son and intended successor of Hadrian

observances.text: Christian feast day: Basil the Great (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Feast of the Circumcision of Christ Feast of the Holy Name of Jesus (Anglican Communion, Lutheran Church)
Christian feast day: Solemnity of Mary, Mother of God; and its related observances: World Day of Peace
The eighth of the Twelve Days of Christmas (Western Christianity)
